title: Session Management — Sessions, Clips, Naming, Stages
problem: A jam night produces 3-8 clips. Band members need to find, name, and navigate them easily. The recorder needs to delete mistakes. After many sessions, the band needs to track which songs are developed vs. still just ideas.
analysis: Sessions and clips stored in Postgres. A session auto-creates on first record of the night (date-based name). Each clip is one song — 5-30 min raw. Clips auto-named using a death metal word generator (adjective + noun pairs from curated lists, e.g. 'Putrid Descent', 'Severed Throne') — more memorable and distinguishable than 'Song 1 - 9:41pm'. Any band member can rename a clip via inline tap-to-edit on the clip name. A shuffle button (small icon next to the name) generates a fresh death metal name and saves it immediately — lets the band re-roll if the auto-name doesn't fit. ClipCard shows a colored stage chip in the meta row (idea=gray, sketch=blue, developing=amber, demo-ready=green) only on non-frozen clips — frozen clips communicate final state with the lock icon instead. ClipCard shows processing spinner when transcodeStatus=PENDING. Delete via '···' overflow button (replaces undiscoverable long-press). Session page bottom padding accounts for both tab bar height and device safe area inset.
successCriteria:
- Session auto-created on first record of the night
- Session name is editable inline by any member
- Clip auto-named with a memorable death metal name (adjective + noun, e.g. 'Putrid Descent')
- Any band member can rename a clip by tapping the clip name
- Shuffle button next to clip name generates a new death metal name and saves immediately (only on non-frozen clips)
- ClipCard shows animated spinner while transcodeStatus is PENDING
- Delete clip via '···' button → confirmation sheet → delete (RECORDER only)
- Clip list shows: name, raw duration, version count, frozen lock, stage chip, processing spinner
- Stage chip shows on non-frozen clips: colored pill (idea=gray, sketch=blue, developing=amber, demo-ready=green)
- Session has optional notes field (notes icon → bottom sheet editor)
- Clip has song stage field: idea / sketch / developing / demo-ready (default: idea)
- Last clip is never hidden behind the bottom tab bar
- Storage indicator shows Drive quota used by this band's audio files
implementation:
1. app/(auth)/bands/[bandId]/sessions/[sessionId]/page.tsx: server component — loads session + clips via Prisma (includes latest version resultDurationMs for correct display on split children), renders SessionHeaderClient + ClipCard list. Uses pb-[calc(56px+env(safe-area-inset-bottom))] for correct clearance.
2. components/sessions/SessionHeaderClient.tsx: client component — inline name edit, notes bottom sheet, QR code bottom sheet. Sticky with top offset from --upload-banner-h CSS variable. Delegates to two extracted sub-components: SessionNotesSheet (owns draft+saving state, syncs to notes on open) and SessionQrSheet (renders QRCode in BottomSheet — pure UI, no state).
3. components/clips/ClipCard.tsx: clip list row — name (tap to edit inline via useClipRename hook from hooks/useClipRename.ts), shuffle button (small icon, generates new death metal name via generateDeathMetalName() from lib/clipNames.ts + direct PATCH /api/clips/{clipId} + router.refresh(); only shown when !clip.frozen), stage chip in meta row (STAGE_CHIP map: {label, className} per ClipStage; only shown when !clip.frozen), duration (latestResultDurationMs ?? sourceDurationMs so split children show correct virtual duration), version count, frozen lock, processing spinner. '···' button opens delete confirmation sheet. Long-press removed (undiscoverable pattern replaced by explicit button).
   hooks/useClipRename.ts: encapsulates inline rename state (name, editingName, nameInput, saving), PATCH /api/clips/{clipId} call, and keyboard handlers (Enter → confirm, Escape → cancel).
4. app/api/clips/[clipId]/route.ts: GET clip, PATCH (name, stage, generatePublicToken, removePublicToken), DELETE (RECORDER only).
5. lib/clipNames.ts: custom death metal adjective + noun word lists + generateDeathMetalName() using unique-names-generator.
6. lib/utils.ts: generateClipName() delegates to generateDeathMetalName(). formatDuration() for M:SS display. formatPosition() for H:MM:SS clock display. calcResultDuration() for virtual cut preview.

decisions.decision: Death metal auto-names instead of 'Song N - time'
decisions.rationale: Sequential names ('Song 1', 'Song 2') are useless for identifying a specific take days later. Time-based names are hard to remember. Short evocative word pairs from a curated death metal vocabulary (adjectives: Putrid, Severed, Ancient; nouns: Descent, Throne, Ritual) are memorable, distinguishable, and set the right tone for a band app.
decisions.recommendation: unique-names-generator library + custom word lists in lib/clipNames.ts. generateClipName() used on upload finalize.

decisions.decision: '···' overflow button replaces long-press for clip delete
decisions.rationale: Long-press is a non-discoverable interaction pattern — users have no visual affordance to find it. Accidental long-press on a clip name (when trying to rename) could trigger a delete sheet unexpectedly. A visible '···' button is a universal mobile pattern and makes the delete action findable without instruction.
decisions.recommendation: Right side of ClipCard: spinner/lock icon (contextual) + '···' button (always visible). '···' opens the delete confirmation sheet. Card body tap still navigates to clip detail.

decisions.decision: Sessions and clips in Postgres, audio files in Drive
decisions.rationale: Postgres is readable by all band members without Drive folder sharing. Session/clip browsing works immediately for new members.

decisions.decision: Song stage is separate from frozen status
decisions.rationale: 'Frozen' means this recording is done. Stage tracks the song's creative progress, not the file state.
decisions.recommendation: stage field on clips row. Default: 'idea'. Values: idea / sketch / developing / demo-ready.

knownLimitations: Death metal names are random — two clips in a session could get the same name (rare, detectable, user can rename)
Drive storage is under the recorder's account — ~43MB per 30-min clip, 15GB Drive free tier supports ~350 raw clips before cleanup needed
Clip delete requires two steps: Postgres record deletion + Drive file deletion — if one fails, manual reconciliation may be needed
userGuide: Sessions appear in the band's session list sorted by date. Tap a session to see its clips. Tap the session name to rename it. Tap the notes icon to add session notes. Tap the QR icon to show a QR code bandmates can scan to join. Each clip card shows its auto-generated name, duration, version count, stage, and current status. Tap the clip name to rename it inline. Tap the shuffle icon next to the name to roll a new death metal name — saves automatically. Tap the stage chip (idea / sketch / developing / demo-ready) to see the song's creative state; change it from the clip detail. Tap anywhere else on the card to open the clip detail. Tap '···' on a clip to delete it (Recorder-only — requires confirmation).

statusHistory.reason: ClipCard: death metal names, '···' delete button replacing long-press, processing spinner, session page padding fix

revisions.reason: Documented extracted sub-components: SessionNotesSheet + SessionQrSheet extracted from SessionHeaderClient; useClipRename hook extracted from ClipCard.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: ClipCard: added stage chip in meta row (colored pill per ClipStage, hidden when frozen). Added shuffle button next to clip name (re-rolls death metal name via generateDeathMetalName + PATCH, non-frozen only).
